description: Windhoek is Namibia’s capital and largest city. Set in the lush central highlands, Windhoek is the starting point for most Namibian journeys and an ideal place to spend a night or two catching your breath. Highlights include the Art Gallery of Namibia, the Craft Centre and the Independence Memorial Museum.

Located in the scenic Namib-Naukluft National Park, Sossusvlei is where you will find the iconic red sand dunes of the Namib. The clear blue skies contrast with the giant red dunes to make this one of the most scenic natural wonders of Africa and a photographer's heaven. This awe-inspiring destination is possibly Namibia's premier attraction, with its unique dunes rising to almost 400 metres - making them some of the highest in the world.

Swakopmund is a coastal city in Namibia, west of the capital, Windhoek. Its sandy beaches face the Atlantic Ocean. Established by German colonists in 1892, the city’s colonial landmarks include the Woermannhaus, a 1905 tower with panoramic views. The city is known for its German influence and is a popular destination for both relaxation and adventure activities.

Set in the Kunene Region of northwestern Namibia, Twyfelfontein is a spectacularly scenic area, featuring one of the largest and most important concentrations of rock art in Africa. Recognized as a UNESCO World Heritage site, it is home to over 2500 engravings. The area is also famous for its geological wonders like the Organ Pipes and Burnt Mountain.

Etosha National Park is one of the most important wildlife sanctuaries in Africa. Its heart is the Etosha Pan, a giant salt crust that is so large it can be seen from space. Surrounding the pan are diverse habitats with an incredible variety of game, including four of the Big Five – elephant, lion, leopard and rhino. The park's numerous waterholes provide excellent opportunities for wildlife viewing.

Located in central Namibia, the Waterberg Plateau National Park is a massive sandstone table mountain that rises to almost 200 metres. The park is home to many rare and endangered species, including black and white rhino, buffalo, sables and roan antelope. The plateau is essentially a 'lost world' of wild animals and lush vegetation, providing a stark contrast to the surrounding plains.
Final check-out from Waterberg Plateau Lodge and self-drive transfer to Hosea Kutako International Airport [WDH] for your departure flight.
